title: A Protocol for Producing Quality Control Materials for Hematology Testing Using Porcine and Goose Blood
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bbmu4","text":"Commercial hematology internal quality control (IQC) materials are often costly, imported, and have limited shelf life, which poses a significant challenge for laboratories in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). This protocol presents a low-cost, locally adaptable method for preparing IQC materials targeting white blood cell (WBC) parameters. Using porcine and goose blood sourced from legal commercial slaughterhouses, the samples are chemically fixed through a two-step stabilization process to preserve cellular morphology and WBC stability over 60 days. The method has been optimized for the Abbott Cell-Dyn Emerald 22 analyzer and validated across other platforms, including Sysmex and Horiba systems, making it applicable in diverse laboratory settings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Goose blood collected from healthy geese (\u003e5 months old), obtained post-mortem from licensed slaughterhouses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b85ny","text":"  - Porcine blood obtained from commercial sources, collected immediately after slaughter.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qhtyw","text":"Anticoagulant: CPDA-1 (Citrate Phosphate Dextrose Adenine)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xo5u6","text":"Buffers and reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cbczw","text":"  - 50 mL sterile centrifuge tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"guhwh","text":"  - Refrigerated centrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vhdt5","text":"  - Magnetic stirrer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ybink","text":"  - Water bath (set to 40°C)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bleyf","text":"  - Glass containers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9dk36","text":"  - Pipettes and standard lab consumables","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Collect goose blood into sterile 50 mL tubes containing CPDA-1 anticoagulant.
Centrifuge at 2000 rpm for 15 minutes to separate plasma.
Wash the erythrocyte pellet 2–3 times with PBS until clean.
Prepare stabilizing solution: 25% glutaraldehyde with 0.44 g/L neomycin sulfate.
Resuspend washed erythrocytes in the stabilizing solution and stir gently on a magnetic stirrer for 1 hour.
Wash fixed erythrocytes 2–3 times with distilled water until the supernatant becomes clear.
Suspend the final product in 0.85% sodium chloride and store at 2–8°C until use.
Mix porcine blood with hemolysis solution at a 1:9 (v/v) ratio.
Add acetic acid to achieve a final concentration of 0.1%.
Incubate the mixture at 40°C for 10 minutes.
Centrifuge at 3000 rpm for 5 minutes and discard the supernatant.
Wash the sediment with PBS, repeat centrifugation until the solution becomes clear (removal of erythrocyte debris).
Suspend ~5 mL of sediment in 20 mL PBS.
Add fixation solution at a 1:4 (v/v) ratio.
Transfer to a glass container and shake gently at room temperature overnight.
After incubation, divide into 50 mL centrifuge tubes and centrifuge again at 3000 rpm for 5 minutes. Wash the pellet 2–3 times with PBS and suspend in 30 mL PBS for preservation. Store at 2–8°C.
creator: My Tran Thai
